svg.text: Item
Virtual objects players can obtain, use, or equip via inventory
I. Definition of Items
Items are virtual objects that players can obtain, use, or equip during gameplay.
Categorized into multiple types and managed uniformly through the inventory system.
II. Item Classification
Equipment
Wearable items
Materials
Crafting resources
Consumables
Usable items
Very Special Items
III. Editing Items
Item tab in Combat Preset; click "New Item" to create
Item Name:
Displayed in interface during gameplay
Item Icon:
Icon displayed in interface
Configuration ID:
Unique identifier of the item template, used in Node Graphs
IV. Basic Settings
Display info and inventory behavior
Rarity
Determines icon background color: gray, green, blue, purple, orange
Stack Limit
Maximum number of items in a single inventory slot
Associated Item Node Graph
Node Graph attached to character when item enters inventory
Inventory Ownership Tab
Determines which tab the item is sorted into in inventory
Description
Item description displayed in interface during gameplay
Has Currency Value / Display Currency Value / Currency Value
Relates currency value for shop transactions
V. Drop Settings
Logic when items drop from inventory into scene
Destruction Debris (when owner is defeated)
Loot
Items converted into loot entities in the scene
Destroy
Items are destroyed on owner defeat
Save
Saved in inventory (only meaningful for characters)
Loot Drop Mechanics
Shared Reward
All players share loot; once picked up, unavailable to others
VI. Interaction Settings
What players can do with items in inventory
Destructible
Whether players can destroy this item in inventory via interface
Tradable
Whether the item can be sold in the shop
Usable
Whether players can use the item through interface actions
Allow Bulk Use
Whether multiple items can be used in a single operation
Auto Use Upon Pickup
When "Yes", item auto-used on acquisition
Cooldown (s)
Time before item can be used again
Group Cooldown System
Group Cooldown (s)
All items in Linked Cooldown Group enter cooldown simultaneously
Linked Cooldown Group: Configure a list of items that defines all items within the cooldown group.
VII. Tutorial: Creating a Destructible Mechanism That Drops Items
Feature: Create a destructible mechanism that drops fowl when destroyed
Create loot item: Prefab Library > Loot > Preset > select model (e.g. "Fowl") > Save
Loot Component: Pickup Rules = "Interactive Pickup", Enable Drop Animation = "Enable"
Create item: Combat Preset > Items > New Item. Set icon, name, type, rarity (green). Inventory Category = Consumable
Item Details: Drop Settings = "Drop", Loot Type = "Shared Loot", Corresponding Loot = select "Fowl" loot
Create mechanism prefab (e.g. Finchball). Add Fixed Loot Component: Group Drop, Shared Loot, Fowl x3
Add Collision Trigger. Node graph: "When Entering Collision Trigger" -> Destroy Entity (self)
Place prefab in scene, enter Test Mode to verify drops
VIII. Tutorial: Picking Up Loot and Using Items to Restore HP
Feature: Pick up dropped fowl, store in inventory, use to restore HP
Add Inventory Component to character via Combat Preset > Edit Character > Components
Configure Inventory Template: Advanced Editing > Open Inventory Template Editor > Add Template
Press [B] during gameplay to open inventory. Loot items are collected via interaction.
Configure item usage logic: Auxiliary Editor > Item Node Graph > "Fowl: Restores HP"
Node graph: "When Items Used" -> Binary Branch (check item ID = Fowl) -> Identify inventory owner
Multiply item quantity for total HP restoration -> Directly Restore HP execution node
Mount node graph: Combat Preset > Items > Fowl > Associated Item Node Graph > select "Fowl: Restores HP"
Note: Loot cannot be picked up without an Inventory Component mounted on the character.
For loot placed directly in stage, configure Loot Content to enable interaction. For drops from destroyed objects, use Fixed Loot Component.
Tip: Individualized Reward gives each player an independent copy of dropped loot.
Players' pickup actions do not affect each other. Use Shared Reward for competitive loot scenarios.
